meaning: vt.过大而不适于,出生,长出,年久丧失(某种习惯,兴趣等)
examples: The girl may or may not outgrow her interest in fashion.
Very often, we do not completely outgrow that infantile notion that our wishes cause things to happen.
As trees outgrow their ideal habitats in the face of climate change, these flying ecosystem engineers could be a big help in replanting trees.
Bouchard won't predict whether fast food and other add-ons will enable him to outgrow 7-Eleven.
Not a lot of people know this, but you never outgrow your need for vaccines.
